reviews.review_description: This is a clever mixed-reality VR game where you have to catch and hold onto whatever a god drops on you from the clouds. The setup is simple, but it works really well in VR and feels fresh right away. A floating, disembodied head hovers nearby, reacting to everything you do, sometimes making fun of you and sometimes praising you, which adds a lot of personality without being too much. The objects range from harmless to slightly creepy, like snakes or spiders, adding a bit of tension to the experience. It is easy to jump into and works well at a party or family gathering since everyone can quickly take a turn. It is quirky in its own way and should be a hit at parties, especially if you cast it so everyone can watch the player react in real time!

reviews.review_title: Stupid fun, and better than expected
reviews.review_description: It has a theme song! This is quite the hidden gem, obviously from the trailer you know random objects drop from the sky and you catch them in your hands but it works way better than I had imagined. Great variety of objects, like a cat, a frog, an anvil, a snake! and random things happen like a fan spawns blowing wind. It relies on metas hand tracking so make sure the lighting is good and your cameras can see your hands and if you switch to VR mode you get a better idea of how your hands work (you got a bit of your arms too, at one point I rolled a basketball down my arm and I was like woah). There's also a sandbox where you can spawn the objects and play with them. Nice work solo dev! You thought of a fun use for mixed reality
This is something else,great physics great graphics would deffo recommend
Best Game with a floppy cat 5 stars would flop the cat again
Fun for the whole fam!!! Very challenging and unique!

A mix of physical challenge and offbeat humor: hold unexpected objects (slippery, heavy, slimy) while facing storms, mischievous cats, or flying spiders.
The real appeal of the game is its replayability and joyfully unpredictable atmosphere: each session offers a new variation, ideal for challenging your friends.
A mini-game brilliantly calibrated for strong emotions... yet lighthearted. Perfect for quick sessions, shared fun, and guaranteed laughs.
Technically perfect with ultra-realistic graphics and physics, it's a favorite!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the amazing feedback! As a small indie studio, reading reviews like yours truly makes our day. We are glad you are enjoying the fun chaos! Stay tuned because we have an update coming very soon!

reviews.review_description: This is my new favorite way to demonstrate VR to my friends and family.  The concept is so simple but the execution is brilliant.  All you have to do is hold the items that fall from the clouds.  It seems so easy but it becomes more challenging as more objects fall and different devices try to knock the objects out of your hands.  The tongue in cheek humor of God berating you as you drop items adds to the fun.  Thank you for making such an inventive game. I highly recommend it.

Fun game that could use some tightening up. Objects could use better hand tracking. Or the tracking surface area is too small. I know the object of the game is to see how much stuff you can hold but the items themselves clip and fall through items. It has a fun jenk to it but would love to see it be better. 
Great sense of humor and overall is a fun pick up and play type of game. Good way to show off MXR to friends
This is a cool mixed reality game with a great use of hand tracking. Crazy items drop from the sky while you try to catch and balance them in your hands. Absolutely hilarious!

reviews.review_title: Great idea that could be a lot of fun!
reviews.review_description: Very novel concept! The world and narrative are fun as well. However, lots of clipping of objects and the hand recognition, I think, needs tuning. Definitely would give the update a play and rank higher!
